feature: Fix review comments on PR #1 and respond to reviewers
description: Исправь замечания по ревью по реквесту номер один и ответь ревьюерам в комментарии.
workflow_rationale: PR #1 cannot be located in the current repository state. The GitHub CLI is not authenticated, and initial investigation shows no open or closed pull requests. This task requires investigation first to: (1) locate PR #1 or determine if it needs to be created, (2) extract all review comments, (3) categorize them by type and complexity, (4) determine the actual scope of changes needed. Only after discovery can implementation proceed.

phases.name: PR Discovery and Documentation
phases.description: Locate PR #1 in the repository and document all review comments requiring action

subtasks.description: Authenticate GitHub CLI and verify repository access
subtasks.notes: BLOCKED: GitHub CLI authentication failed. Current GITHUB_TOKEN is invalid. User must run 'gh auth login' manually to authenticate. This is an interactive process that requires browser OAuth or manual token input. See subtask-1-1-blocker.md for detailed instructions.

verification.command: gh pr view 1 || gh pr list --state all | head -20
verification.expected: PR found or confirmation of PR list

subtasks.notes: INVESTIGATION COMPLETE: Created INVESTIGATION.md documenting findings. PR #1 cannot be located due to GitHub CLI authentication issues. Evidence shows PR #2 exists (commit 3cfa91e), confirming PRs exist in this repository. No local references to PR #1 found. BLOCKED: Requires GitHub CLI authentication (gh auth login) to access PR information. See INVESTIGATION.md for complete details.

subtasks.description: Extract and document all review comments from PR #1

verification.instructions: Review review_comments.json contains all review comments with: reviewer name, file path, line number, comment content, and suggested action

subtasks.notes: Document each review comment in a structured JSON format for tracking. Include: id, reviewer, file, line, content, type (code-fix/doc/clarification/style).

subtasks.expected_output: Document with: (1) PR #1 status and location, (2) Complete list of review comments, (3) Categorization by type, (4) Affected files and services, (5) Estimated complexity for each fix

verification.instructions: Review INVESTIGATION.md contains complete findings including PR status, all review comments categorized, affected files, and complexity estimates

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created comprehensive INVESTIGATION.md document with: (1) PR #1 status analysis, (2) Review comment categorization framework (code-fix, documentation, clarification, style, other), (3) Affected services mapping (main, tinyclaw_integration, claw_compactor, documentation), (4) Complexity estimation guidelines (simple, moderate, complex), (5) Implementation roadmap, (6) Templates for review_comments.json and response_drafts.md. Document is blocked pending GitHub CLI authentication but provides complete framework for when PR #1 becomes accessible.

phases.name: Review Comment Analysis
phases.description: Analyze review comments and create implementation plan for each

subtasks.description: Categorize review comments by type and priority

verification.instructions: Verify INVESTIGATION.md has categorized comments into: code-fix (must fix), documentation (should fix), clarification (respond only), style (nice to have)

subtasks.notes: Group comments to determine implementation order. Code-fix comments are highest priority.

subtasks.description: Identify files requiring changes and map to services

verification.instructions: Verify INVESTIGATION.md has mapping of each file to its service (tinyclaw_integration, claw_compactor, etc.)

subtasks.notes: Create a mapping so we know which service each file belongs to for targeted fixes.

verification.instructions: Verify INVESTIGATION.md documents any conflicting feedback and proposed resolution strategy

subtasks.notes: If reviewers disagree on approach, document the conflict and propose a resolution.

phases.name: Implement Review Fixes
phases.description: Implement fixes for all code-fix review comments

files_to_modify: To be determined from review comments
subtasks.notes: Fix code issues identified in review comments. Follow existing patterns in the codebase.

subtasks.description: Implement fixes for code-fix comments (batch 2 - claw compactor)
files_to_modify: To be determined from review comments
subtasks.notes: Fix code issues in claw-compactor integration. Follow existing patterns.

subtasks.description: Update documentation based on review comments
files_to_modify: To be determined from review comments

verification.instructions: Verify documentation is accurate, complete, and addresses reviewer feedback

subtasks.notes: Update any README files, docstrings, or comments as requested in review.

phases.name: Respond to Reviewers
phases.description: Add response comments to PR thread for each review item

subtasks.description: Draft response comments for all review items

verification.instructions: Verify response_drafts.md contains a response for each review comment following the template: '@reviewer - Thank you! [explanation] Fixed in commit: [hash]'

subtasks.notes: Draft all responses before posting. Use respectful tone and reference specific commits.

subtasks.description: Post response comments to PR thread
subtasks.notes: Use 'gh pr comment [pr-number] --body "response"' to add responses. Post each drafted response.

subtasks.description: Mark resolved review comments as resolved

verification.instructions: Verify all applicable review comments are marked as resolved in the PR

subtasks.notes: For each addressed comment, mark it as resolved in the GitHub PR interface.

phases.name: Verification and Testing
phases.description: Verify all fixes work correctly and no regressions introduced

subtasks.description: Run full test suite on affected services

verification.expected: All tests pass with acceptable coverage

subtasks.notes: Run all tests to ensure no regressions. Check coverage for new code.

steps: Verify each code fix matches reviewer intent
Test the specific functionality changed by each fix
Check for any side effects or unintended changes

subtasks.notes: Manually verify each fix addresses the reviewer's concern without breaking other functionality.

subtasks.description: Verify PR is in mergeable state

verification.expected: PR is mergeable and has no outstanding review requests

subtasks.notes: Confirm the PR has no conflicts, all tests pass, and reviewers are satisfied.

acceptance_criteria: PR #1 located and review comments documented in INVESTIGATION.md
All code-fix comments implemented with changes
All review comments have response comments in PR thread
Unit tests pass for all affected services
No regressions detected in existing functionality

verification_steps.name: Discovery Complete
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Document contains PR status, all review comments, categorization, and affected files

verification_steps.name: PR Status Check
verification_steps.expected_outcome: PR is mergeable and reviewDecision is APPROVED or no outstanding requests

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk task requires unit and integration test coverage. Review comment fixes must not break existing functionality. Verification requires both automated tests and manual review of fixes.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution required - each phase depends on previous phase completion

notes: This is primarily an investigation workflow - implementation cannot start until PR #1 is located
GitHub CLI authentication is required (gh auth login)
If PR #1 does not exist, escalate for task clarification
All fixes must follow existing codebase patterns from worktrees 001 and 002
Each subtask requires verification before proceeding to next

checks: response_drafts.md contains responses for all comments
All review comments have author responses
